_readme: Generated by build_voice_manifest.js. Feed to generate_voices.py.

voiceHints.Bellow: booming gravel-voiced arena announcer, theatrical, middle-aged man
voiceHints.Quarry Boy: young man, shaken but defiant, rural accent
voiceHints.Veiled Woman: woman 30s, desperate dignity, voice cracks under control
voiceHints.Brakka: deep gruff orc mercenary, terse, dry
voiceHints.Vexa: young woman, fast manic gleeful energy, smirking
voiceHints.Dorian: earnest formal knight, warm baritone, self-deprecating
voiceHints.Faelar: serene elf, unhurried, dry wit, ageless
voiceHints.Sylvara: cold precise aristocratic woman, cutting consonants
voiceHints.Pippa: bright halfling woman, relentlessly cheerful, quick

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: No banner, no name. I'll make it quick, little nobody.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Left or right, nobody? Pick which of us kills you.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: The sand inside my circle belongs to me.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: The Pit was a quarry. I will make it a kiln.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: The Pit banned powder. The Pit also takes my bribes.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Swing wildly, please. My blade prefers volunteers.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Break them, nobody. I'll just sew faster.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: They haven't been fed, nobody. Apologies.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: The Pit buries its losers shallow. Convenient for me.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Patch the hounds, Sawbones. Let the famous one tire himself.
lines.note: board taunt: THE KENNEL & THE NEEDLE

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: You shoot, I raise. The Pit loves a partnership.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Little nobody got bigger. The Door got friends.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Swing wildly, please. My associates insist.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Stand behind me, Saint. Make the legend come to us.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Fire, frost, or lightning — pick your last word.
lines.note: board taunt: THE COVEN OF KILNS

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Cut him down. We'll have him standing before you catch your breath.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Swing and I counter. Hesitate and he fires. Choose.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: This isn't a fight, nobody. It's a demolition.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: I know what the Pit gave you, nobody. It gave it to me first.

lines.speaker: BELLOW
lines.text: Bellow, sweating: "You weren't supposed to get this far.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The wandering samurai — said to have never lost a duel. He has traveled from the far east to become the greatest swordsman in the world. No magic. No levels. No enchantments or tricks. Just pure skill, and the vigor of defeated foes.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: From a grove far away, she comes seeking what nature could not teach her. She hates the city — but she has strength to prove, and there is no better place than the kitchen… I mean, the arena…

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A dark elf from even darker realms, beneath the dark recesses of the land. His book is disconcerting. His staff is ominous. More lurks behind his portals than the city guard should logically allow. Welp — too late. He's in the arena.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Bellow chalks a line through the word NOBODY.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: "Sand's cheap," someone says. "Names are cheaper."

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The crowd is already betting on the next one.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The Door nods once. Professional respect.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Your katana is sold at auction by Thursday. It refuses to stay sold.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The pit rats eat well tonight. Bellow charges them admission.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: "He almost had a name," a child says. Almost is the Pit's favorite word.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Wager slips fall from the tiers like grey snow. Yours among them.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The Stitcher offers to sew you back together. Bellow asks who pays.

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: You want to know about Dren? Last champion before you. "Ran off," Bellow says. Dren left his winnings in my strongbox and his boots under the bed. Men who run, run IN boots.

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: The rest isn't free. My ears cost me good coin to keep open. Five silver and you'll know what the road knows.

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: Three more vanished off the trade road this season. A juggler, a hedge-witch, a quarry boy who could lift a cart. All GIFTED, you follow? And a guest upstairs keeps asking after your fights — what you do, how fast, how often. Asked after Dren the same way, the week before he vanished. I'd visit the guild before the road, champion.

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: Five silver. The Pit paid you a silver a head — don't tell me you're short.

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: Watch the alleys, champion. Whatever shops for the gifted hasn't finished its list.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Tents that fold fast. Crates with air-holes. A cold fire pit and a cage with bent bars. This is a waystation, not a camp.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Wheel ruts where no wagon should fit between the trees. The ruts are deep. The cargo is heavy, or alive, or both.

lines.speaker: THE QUARRY BOY
lines.text: He comes up swinging until he sees the cage door open. "They watched me lift at the quarry fair. WEEKS ago. Asked the others what I could do, wrote it down. There's a buyer in the city — back alleys, near the west wall. I heard them price me." He won't take an escort. He runs.

lines.speaker: SHEN SAMA
lines.text: Scales like cooling slag under a traveler's hood. Eyes too old for the face. "You freed the wrong cargo, champion — they'll re-profile and restock within a season. They are SHOPPING. Specific gifts, specific blood. Mine most of all." He looks north, the way hunted things do. "Don't follow me. Being near me is how people end up in crates." He is gone between one breath and the next.

lines.speaker: THE VEILED WOMAN
lines.text: A back-alley meeting gone wrong the moment you aren't who she expected. She doesn't run. "You broke the camp. Then you've killed her — the elixir was BOUGHT, paid in full. Out of the Ashenveil, they said. Grown, they said, not brewed." She shows you the vial — it hums against your teeth like the ley-node did.

lines.speaker: THE VEILED WOMAN
lines.text: "I don't care what it's made of." Her voice cracks on the lie. "I was told it's the only one that works. You want the men who sell it? They come down the west forest path, new moon, every month. That's all I know. Leave me the vial. Please."

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: You keep the vial. Evidence. She doesn't cry where you can see it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: You leave her the vial. Whatever it is, whoever it was — tonight it's mercy. The trail is still the trail.

lines.speaker: ANKUNYX
lines.text: He comes through Karridge the way weather comes — the plaza kneels in a wave, the pit-criers go silent mid-shout. ANKUNYX. The Dragon Emperor. He is exactly as tall as the stories and somehow worse: calm. His eyes pass over the crowd and stop, briefly, on you.

lines.speaker: ANKUNYX
lines.text: "The pit-crowned." His voice is conversational, which is the frightening part. "Karridge is louder about you than about its taxes. Good. Loud places stay honest." A breath. You hold a vial that hums, a ledger page, the names of three freed captives who begged you not to speak theirs. Accuse the Emperor's own house — with THIS? He is already turning away. The crowd is already rising. The moment is already over.

lines.speaker: ANKUNYX
lines.text: The conspiracy holds. The camp is ash and silence; the Academy's lower levels keep their secrets; somewhere a woman with a vial tells herself it's medicine. But the captives are free, the grove's line runs clean — and you know. The crowd still chants your name. The story is not over. It has barely begun.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Word ran ahead of itself for two days: the Dragon Emperor passes through Karridge. The plaza fills at dawn. Criers clear the road. The guild polishes its ledger table. Bellow, for the first time in living memory, wears a clean shirt.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He does not come. The road north stays empty past noon, past the bells, past the point where the crowd stops pretending it isn't cold. A steward's rider finally trots in with the official word: the Emperor's itinerary has changed. No reason given. There never is. The crowd grumbles and disperses, and a hundred wagers die unpaid. Somewhere behind you, Bellow mutters: "Twenty-five years, and that man has never once been where anyone expects him to be."

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: You stand a while in the emptying plaza, the vial humming in your coat, the crowd's name for you drifting back and forth across the stones. There was no one to show the evidence to today. You tell yourself that's why you're smiling — half a smile, the kind a man keeps for his own secrets. The conspiracy holds. The hunt continues. And the Pit's champion polishes a blade that has worn three names, and answers, as ever, to none of them.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Three days by coach, two changes of horses, one toll bridge where the guard waves you through when he recognizes the pit-name. Varenholm: spires, banners, streetlamps with GLASS in them. The Crown Quarter smells like money and rosin. Playbills on every post: ONE NIGHT — THE DANCER, V.E.A. MASTER OF VOICE — CIVIC AUDITORIUM.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The Civic Auditorium is full past the fire-marshal's patience. She comes out small and the room gets smaller — a halfling in firebird red, scroll-marks on her shoulders catching the footlights gold. The dance is all gyration and joy and impossible precision, and then the EMOTION arrives: not heard, FELT, washing the tiers like warm water. Two thousand strangers grinning at once and none of them knowing why.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: You know why. You've felt a hum like this before — in a vial, in a node, under a grove. Hers is tuned to the heart where the grove's is tuned to the green, but it is the SAME instrument. Somewhere in this city, you'd wager a ledger has four small letters next to her name.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: You know why — and it is worse than knowing. Her hum reaches the tiers and BRUSHES YOURS, string against string, and for half a beat the dancer misses a step no one else notices. From the stage, mid-spin, two thousand faces deep, she finds you. Her grin does not falter. Her eyes say: huh.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The coach south leaves at dawn. Varenholm's glass lamps go gold behind you. Somewhere in the Crown Quarter a dancer who is exactly as hunted as she is famous takes a third encore, because the best place to hide from the dark is the absolute center of the light.

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: She's at the guild board before you are, still in the red, hair pinned with what is definitely a stolen quill. "You're the pit one. Karridge. They do a CHANT about you, it's very dramatic." She doesn't wait for an answer. "I'm Cookie. I'm taking the saltcellar job and I need a front line. You're hired. I pay in exposure and one (1) gold."

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: She's at the guild board before you are — and she goes still the moment you're close, head tilting like a tuning fork. "...Huh. There it IS. You were at the show. Third tier, left. You HUM." She looks you up and down with frank delight. "Green-flavored. Mine's all heartstrings. Okay, two questions: saltcellar job, you in? And does dad know about YOU too, or is he still collecting us by accident?"

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: "Saltcellar by the canal. Something's chewing through the pork shipments and the warehouse boys won't go down past the third step. Guild says rats. Rats don't leave FROST on the railings. Probably wights. Possibly worse. Definitely fun."

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: "See, THAT'S a front line." She flips a silver to the warehouse boy watching from the stairs. "Drinks would be on me, except—"

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: "—FOUR of you this time? I'm FLATTERED." She straightens her hair-quill, breathing hard, grinning harder. "They keep sending little shopping parties. One day someone's going to explain to them what happened to the wererats." A beat — the grin banks down to something older. "You broke one of their camps out east. I heard. They don't stop, you know. They just re-letter the ledger." She presses the promised gold into your hand and one extra. "For the warning you didn't have to ride three days to give me. Tell the road I said hi."

lines.speaker: COOKIE
lines.text: "—FOUR of you this time? I'm FLATTERED." She catches her breath, then looks at you — really looks. "They had two cart-cages, cousin. TWO." The grin banks down to something older. "Whatever we are, we're worth more to them alive, which means we never stop being worth it. So: rule one, never be boring. Rule two, never be ALONE when it matters." She presses the gold into your hand, plus one extra, plus — quickly, like it costs her — a squeeze of your wrist. "Family discount. If you ever find out whether dad knows... I get the letter first."

lines.speaker: MARLOW
lines.text: Three more vanished off the trade road this season. A juggler, a hedge-witch, a quarry boy who could lift a cart. All GIFTED, you follow? And a guest upstairs keeps asking after your fights — what you do, how fast, how often. Asked after Dren the same way, the week before he vanished. I'd visit the guild before the road, champion. ...You'd know about gifted, I'd wager. The way the herb-pots on my sill lean toward you when you walk past. No offense, champion.

lines.speaker: SHEN SAMA
lines.text: Scales like cooling slag under a traveler's hood. Eyes too old for the face. "You freed the wrong cargo, champion — they'll re-profile and restock within a season. They are SHOPPING. Specific gifts, specific blood. Mine most of all." He looks north, the way hunted things do. "Don't follow me. Being near me is how people end up in crates." He is gone between one breath and the next. He stops, half-turned. Nostrils flare, like catching woodsmoke. "...And yours, sister. Verdance, if I had to guess — life-magic gone loud. You don't even know what you're carrying, do you?" Something almost gentle crosses the old eyes. "Stay away from the Ashenveil. ESPECIALLY you. What they'd render you down for is the one thing they can't brew."

lines.speaker: THE VEILED WOMAN
lines.text: A back-alley meeting gone wrong the moment you aren't who she expected. She doesn't run. "You broke the camp. Then you've killed her — the elixir was BOUGHT, paid in full. Out of the Ashenveil, they said. Grown, they said, not brewed." She shows you the vial — it hums against your teeth like the ley-node did. For you it is worse: the vial hums in your TEETH, in the wet of your blood, the way the grove node hums — the way, you realize with a cold drop, that YOU hum. You have always thought everyone could hear the green singing. They can't.

lines.speaker: ANKUNYX
lines.text: "The pit-crowned." His voice is conversational, which is the frightening part. "Karridge is louder about you than about its taxes. Good. Loud places stay honest." A breath. You hold a vial that hums, a ledger page, the names of three freed captives who begged you not to speak theirs. Accuse the Emperor's own house — with THIS? He is already turning away. The crowd is already rising. The moment is already over. And then he stops. Not on the vial. On YOU. For three heartbeats the Emperor of everything looks at you the way a man looks at a song he heard once, years ago, in a town whose name he traded away. He almost frowns. He moves on. He does not know what he is leaving in the plaza — and you, who have your mother's caution if not her contentment, do not tell him.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: They knew your routes. They were waiting off the path with sedative oils, a reinforced cart, and a ledger page bearing one word in scribe's shorthand: VERDANCE. Underlined. Twice.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The cart burns. The chains go into the river. But the ledger page is the part you keep: they did not come hunting a pit champion. They came shopping — and you are the listed item. The grove keeper was right. The line runs thin because something is drinking from it, and whatever it is has now tasted your name.

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "You're the pit-crowned. Saw your third fight. Sloppy footwork, good instincts." He goes back to oiling the axe. "Mercenary company's gone. Just me. Contracts only."

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "Guild pays on time. That's all the poetry I need."

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "Wolves in the grove move like they're herded. Herded means a herder."

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "You fight like the Pit taught you. The Pit teaches ugly. Works though."

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "The hollow under the grove. My company's last contract went in there, years back. One of mine never came out — Dorga. If the thing that kept that place is dead..." he stops oiling. "Is it dead?"

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "Then Dorga's debt is paid and I'm out of reasons to sit here." He stands. The axe looks lighter on his shoulder than it should. "Contract's simple: I walk where you walk. Pay negotiable. Don't waste my time and we'll be fine."

lines.speaker: BRAKKA
lines.text: "Come back when it's done. I don't move on maybes."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "Ooh. You smell like the Pit and bad decisions." She doesn't look up from a vial that is, faintly, smoking. "I'm not selling anything. Legally."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "The well water FIZZES if you add saltpeter. Nobody appreciates this but me."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "The guild board's rot shaman? Its residue would make a GORGEOUS accelerant."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "If anyone asks, the alley smelled like that when I got here."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "YES. Okay. Rules: I walk where you walk, I light what you point at, and if I say 'run' it's already too late. This is going to be GREAT."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "Keep your hum-bottle then. I'll be here. Bored. DANGEROUSLY bored."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "Wait. WAIT. That vial — it HUMS. Do you know what I could learn from— okay. Trade. You give me the hum-vial, I give you me. Field alchemy, fire support, sparkling conversation. That's a steal and you know it."

lines.speaker: VEXA
lines.text: "You gave the real one away? To a SAD WOMAN? Ugh, you're one of THOSE." She grins, all teeth. "Fine. Bring me a strength potion to take apart instead. I'll come along for the entertainment value alone."

lines.speaker: DORIAN
lines.text: "Champion." He salutes with a hand that remembers better banners. "Dorian. Formerly of the Third Shields. The 'formerly' is the interesting part, and I'd rather not."

lines.speaker: DORIAN
lines.text: "The missing folk on the road — someone should stand for them. Someone with no reputation to lose, perhaps."

lines.speaker: DORIAN
lines.text: "I drill at dawn behind the inn. Habit. The sword forgives routine, never absence."

lines.speaker: FAELAR
lines.text: "The grove has opinions about visitors. About you it is... curious. That's rarer than approval."

lines.speaker: FAELAR
lines.text: "The node hums clean again since you broke their camp. The trees mention it. Frequently. They're worse than gossips."

lines.speaker: FAELAR
lines.text: "Deepwood's Eldest know more than they sing. They always do."

lines.speaker: SYLVARA
lines.text: "You're louder than you think, champion. So is what you're hunting." She doesn't turn around. "I was Academy. Ashenveil. Ask better questions than the guild does."

lines.speaker: SYLVARA
lines.text: "The lower levels have a smell. Copper and lilac. You never stop noticing it."

lines.speaker: SYLVARA
lines.text: "They're not brewing potions down there. They're farming. Choose your words by their teeth."

lines.speaker: PIPPA
lines.text: "OH. Oh you're the one! I bet two silver on you in fight nine and bought these boots. LOOK at these boots."

lines.speaker: PIPPA
lines.text: "There's a chest in the grove nobody's found. Well. Nobody's found TWICE."

lines.speaker: PIPPA
lines.text: "Dungeon under the roots, treasure at the bottom — I'd have gotten to it eventually. Probably. The skeletons are a strong argument for 'eventually'."
